ActiveDirectoryAuthenticationProvider.summary: This class implements and is used for active directory federated authentication mechanisms.

ctor2.param: Client Application Id to be used for acquiring an access token for federated authentication. The driver uses its own application client id by default.
ctor2.summary: Initializes the class with the provided application client id.

ctor3.param: The callback method to be used with 'Active Directory Device Code Flow' authentication.
(Optional) Client Application Id to be used for acquiring an access token for federated authentication. The driver uses its own application client id by default.
ctor3.summary: Initializes the class with the provided device code flow callback method and application client id.

ctorOptions.param: A instance whose properties initialize the provider. Must not be .
ctorOptions.summary: Initializes the class from a bag. This is the recommended constructor for new code because new options can be added to ProviderOptions without forcing breaking changes on callers.

ProviderOptions.summary: Property bag used by the constructor.
ProviderOptions.remarks: Prefer this options-bag constructor over the positional-argument overloads in new code. Additional options can be added here in future versions without introducing new constructor overloads.

AcquireTokenAsync.param: The Active Directory authentication parameters passed to authentication providers.
AcquireTokenAsync.summary: Acquires a security token from the authority.
AcquireTokenAsync.returns: Represents an asynchronous operation that returns the authentication token.

ClearUserTokenCache.summary: Clears cached user tokens from the token provider.

remarks.para: This will cause interactive authentication prompts to appear again if tokens were previously being obtained from the cache.
The driver's per-pool federated-authentication token cache is also cleared, so subsequent SqlConnection.Open calls will reacquire fed-auth tokens instead of reusing cached entries.

SetDeviceCodeFlowCallback.param: The callback method to be used with 'Active Directory Device Code Flow' authentication.
SetDeviceCodeFlowCallback.summary: Sets the callback method, overriding the default implementation that processes the result for 'Active Directory Device Code Flow' authentication.

SetParentActivityOrWindowFunc.param: A function that returns the parent window/activity object to be used by MSAL for interactive authentication UI. On Windows, return an window handle (or an on .NET Framework). On Android, return an Activity; on iOS, return a UIViewController (or the platform equivalent). Pass to clear a previously installed callback and revert to the provider's default behavior.
SetParentActivityOrWindowFunc.summary: Sets a function to return the parent window handle to be used for WAM (Windows Account Manager) broker authentication prompts.
SetParentActivityOrWindowFunc.remarks:
On Windows, this handle is used to parent the WAM broker dialog. If not set, the provider will attempt to automatically detect the console window handle. On non-Windows platforms, this callback is forwarded to MSAL so interactive flows can parent their UI to the host Activity / UIViewController (or platform equivalent).
Exceptions thrown by when it is invoked by MSAL are not caught by the provider; they propagate up to the caller of the originating AcquireToken request so that bugs in the callback surface where they are most diagnosable.

SetIWin32WindowFunc.param: A function to return the current window.
SetIWin32WindowFunc.summary: Sets a reference to the current that triggers the browser to be shown. Used to center the browser pop-up onto this window.
SetIWin32WindowFunc.remarks: This API exists for .NET Framework callers using the embedded WebView. New code on any framework should prefer , which works on both .NET Framework and modern .NET and integrates with the WAM broker on Windows.

SetAcquireAuthorizationCodeAsyncCallback.param: The callback method to be called by MSAL.NET to delegate the Web user interface with the Secure Token Service (STS).
SetAcquireAuthorizationCodeAsyncCallback.summary: Sets a callback method which is invoked with a custom Web UI instance that will let the user sign-in with Entra ID, present consent if needed, and get back the authorization code. Applicable when working with Active Directory Interactive authentication.
SetAcquireAuthorizationCodeAsyncCallback.remarks: The "authorizationUri" is crafted to leverage PKCE in order to protect the token from a man in the middle attack. Only MSAL.NET can redeem the code. In the event of cancellation, the implementer should return .

BeforeLoad.summary: This method is called immediately before the provider is added to authentication provider registry.
BeforeLoad.remarks: Avoid performing long-waiting tasks in this method, since it can block other threads from accessing the provider registry.

BeforeUnload.summary: This method is called immediately before the provider is removed from the authentication provider registry.
BeforeUnload.remarks: For example, this method is called when a different provider with the same authentication method overrides this provider in the authentication provider registry. Avoid performing long-waiting task in this method, since it can block other threads from accessing the provider registry.

IsSupported.summary: Indicates whether the specified authentication method is supported.
IsSupported.returns: if the specified authentication method is supported; otherwise, .
IsSupported.remarks:
The supported authentication modes with are:
Active Directory Password
Active Directory Integrated
Active Directory Interactive
Active Directory Service Principal
Active Directory Device Code Flow
Active Directory Managed Identity
Active Directory MSI
Active Directory Default
